channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Trump revels in mass federal firings and jeers at Biden before adoring conservative crowd
item.description: OXON HILL, Md. (AP) — President Donald Trump said Saturday that “nobody's ever seen anything" like his administration's sweeping effort to fire thousands of federal employees and shrink the size of government, congratulating himself for “dominating” Washington and sending bureaucrats “packing.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT and ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Hawaii man freed after 30 years in prison visits mother's grave and ponders ubiquitous cellphones
item.description: HONOLULU (AP) — One of the first places Gordon Cordeiro visited when a judge ordered him released after spending 30 years in prison for a killing he always maintained he had nothing to do with was his mother’s Hawaii gravesite.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER SINCO KELLEHER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Musk gives all federal workers 48 hours to explain what they did last week
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Hundreds of thousands of federal workers have been given little more than 48 hours to explain what they accomplished over the last week, sparking confusion across key agencies as billionaire Elon Musk expands his crusade to slash the size of federal government.
item.dc:creator: STEVE PEOPLES, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Pennsylvania Gov. Shapiro meets with family of fallen officer, victims of hospital shooting
item.description: "I am inspired by the amazing doctors, nurses, and staff at UPMC and WellSpan Health who did tremendous work to save lives in the face of tragedy," said Shapiro. "It was my honor to meet with them and to thank them for what they do in service to their neighbors.
item.dc:creator: George Stockburger, Fox 5

item.title: Oscar favorite ‘Anora’ wins best film, director and actor at the Independent Spirit Awards
item.description: Sean Baker's “Anora” won best film, best director and best actor for Mikey Madison at the Independent Spirit Awards on Saturday in what could be a preview of next Sunday’s Oscars: The film about a Brooklyn sex worker and her whirlwind affair with a Russian oligarch’s son has emerged in recent weeks
item.dc:creator: LINDSEY BAHR, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Hunter Schafer on why she spoke out about being issued a male passport
item.description: SANTA MONICA, Calif. (AP) — Hunter Schafer says “it's important to just keep track of where things are in our country,” a day after the “Euphoria” star posted a video in which she revealed her new passport had been issued with a male gender marker.
item.dc:creator: KRYSTA FAURIA, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: USDA scholarship for students at historically Black colleges suspended
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal scholarship aimed at boosting students from underserved and rural areas attending historically Black colleges and universities has been put on hold. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Shooting after pursuit at Air Force Base in New Mexico kills an airman and wounds another
item.description: ALBUQUERQUE, N.M. (AP) — A shooting at a U.S. Air Force base in New Mexico early Saturday left one airman dead and another wounded, military officials said, adding that it was not an act of terrorism or an attack by an outsider.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Two Virginia Beach police officers shot dead while 'defenseless' during late-night traffic stop
item.description: Two young officers were killed Friday night during a traffic stop in Virginia Beach. While officers were on the ground "defenseless," the suspect allegedly shot them a second time.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Venezuelan family in US under curtailed humanitarian protections clings to faith amid uncertainty
item.description: HOPKINS, Minn. (AP) — Every Sunday, Johann Teran goes to worship at a Lutheran service in suburban Minneapolis, trying to find some hope that the future he was building isn’t all slipping away.
item.dc:creator: GIOVANNA DELL'ORTO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Killer nightclub hostess's deadly love affair with race car driver was 'eye for an eye': author
item.description: "A Cruel Love: The Ruth Ellis Story," a four-part drama series, was inspired by Carol Ann Lee's true-crime book, "A Fine Day for a Hanging: The Real Ruth Ellis Story.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Roblox is 'hunting ground for child sex predators,' new lawsuit claims
item.description: The lawsuit was filed earlier this month on behalf of a 13-year-old boy who was an "avid user of Roblox and Discord.
item.dc:creator: Amy Larson, NewsNation

item.title: Parents and kids navigate talks of loss and tragedy as they return home after LA area fires
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Ceiba Phillips, 11, couldn't believe what he saw when he returned to his Southern California neighborhood a month after a wildfire reduced it to rubble. The ruins of his best friend’s house and his beloved school.
item.dc:creator: JAIMIE DING, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Woman accused of drugging and robbing older men in a deadly romance scheme
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — A woman used online dating apps to lure at least four older men to meet her in person, then drugged them with sedatives and stole hundreds of thousands of dollars in a “sinister” romance scheme, FBI officials in Las Vegas said Friday.
item.dc:creator: TY ONEIL, JESSE BEDAYN and SEJAL GOVINDARAO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Delta faces first lawsuits days after plane flips in Toronto
item.description: All 21 people injured in the crash landing have been released from the hospital, and no one was killed.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, KRON4

item.title: Tesla recalls over 376,000 vehicles due to power steering defect
item.description: The recall involves some 2023 Tesla Model 3s and some 2023 Tesla Model Ys, according to the recall report from the National Highway Traffic Safety Administration.
item.dc:creator: Russell Falcon, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Roblox is 'hunting ground for child sex predators,' new lawsuit claims
item.description: The lawsuit was filed earlier this month on behalf of a 13-year-old boy who was an "avid user of Roblox and Discord.
item.dc:creator: Amy Larson, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Judge cancels NYC Mayor Eric Adams' trial and leaves corruption charges intact until mid-March
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A federal judge on Friday canceled the corruption trial for New York City Mayor Eric Adams and appointed counsel to advise the court about the Justice Department’s controversial request to drop charges against the Democrat. Judge Dale E.
item.dc:creator: LARRY NEUMEISTER and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Judge upholds ban on DOGE accessing sensitive Treasury information, for now
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A federal judge in Manhattan on Friday extended a ban on Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency accessing sensitive Treasury Department information, but rejected broader restrictions sought by 19 Democratic state attorneys general who sued over the issue. Judge Jeannette A.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Supreme Court won’t allow Trump to immediately fire head of whistleblower office
item.description: The justices said in an unsigned order that Hampton Dellinger, head of the Office of Special Counsel, could remain in his job at least until Feb. 26. That’s when a lower-court order temporarily protecting him expires.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Pentagon says it will cut 5,400 probationary workers starting next week
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Defense Department said Friday that it’s cutting 5,400 probationary workers starting next week and will put a hiring freeze in place.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: New Los Angeles DA Nathan Hochman does not support overturning Menendez brothers' murder convictions
item.description: Newly-elected Los Angeles District Attorney Nathan Hochman said at a Friday press conference that he has asked the court to reject the Menendez brothers' request for a new trial.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Luigi Mangione draws crowd for first court hearing since arraignment in UnitedHealthcare CEO killing
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The defendant wore a bulletproof vest and shackles. A woman in the crowd wore a “Free Luigi" scarf. Outside, throngs of people cheered and chanted his name.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: You're going to see more 'city-killer' asteroids, but fret not, MIT astronomer says
item.description: This week's hype around a "city-killing" asteroid possibly striking Earth raised the fears of many before the odds of impact dropped to nearly nothing, but the MIT scientist who developed the risk scale for this type of event said it still has a lot to teach the public.
item.dc:creator: Cameron Kiszla, KTLA 5

item.title: Luigi Mangione makes first court appearance since arraignment in UnitedHealthcare CEO's death
item.description: Luigi Mangione, 26, has pleaded not guilty to multiple counts of murder, including murder as an act of terrorism, in the Dec. 4 killing of Brian Thompson outside a midtown Manhattan hotel.
item.dc:creator: Michael R. Sisak, Fox 5

item.title: A New Jersey man is convicted of attempted murder in the stabbing of Salman Rushdie
item.description: MAYVILLE, N.Y. (AP) — A New Jersey man was convicted Friday of attempted murder for stabbing author Salman Rushdie multiple times on a New York lecture stage in 2022.
item.dc:creator: CAROLYN THOMPSON, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Judge extends temporary block to huge cuts in National Institutes of Health research funding
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — A federal judge on Friday again blocked the Trump administration’s drastic cuts in medical research funding that many scientists say will endanger patients and delay new lifesaving studies.
item.dc:creator: LAURAN NEERGAARD and MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Los Angeles DA gives update in Menendez brothers' resentencing case
item.description: The Los Angeles district attorney said Friday he hasn't yet made a decision on whether Lyle and Erik Menendez should be resentenced in their 1996 conviction for the murder of their wealthy parents at their Beverly Hills home.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Actress Hunter Schafer says she was issued a male passport
item.description: Hunter Schafer, one of Hollywood’s most visible transgender stars, said she was issued a U.S. passport with a male gender marker after her original passport, which listed her gender as female, was stolen.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: US Marshals Service deputizes Musk's security team: Reports
item.description: The U.S. Marshals Service has deputized some of tech billionaire Elon Musk's private security detail, giving them specific rights and protections of federal law enforcement agents, multiple outlets reported.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, Fox 5

item.title: Dunkin' to remove upcharge for non-dairy milk: reports
item.description: Dunkin' customers will no longer be charged extra for requesting non-dairy milk in their drinks. Beginning March 5, dairy alternatives will be standard options for Dunkin' beverages at no additional cost to guests, a spokesperson from the Massachusetts-based coffee chain told multiple media outlets.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: US housing market worth nearly $50 trillion, over double a decade ago
item.description: The total value of all U.S. homes has doubled over the past decade. These cities saw the fastest growth last year.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, NewsNation

item.title: Speaker Johnson discourages DOGE stimulus checks idea
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) threw cold water on the idea of using savings from the Trump administration’s Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) government-slashing activities to send checks to Americans.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, ABC 4

item.title: Forever 21 may close remaining stores as second bankruptcy looms
item.description: Fashion retailer Forever 21 has begun liquidation sales at several of its locations throughout the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Hallenbeck, WFLA, NBC
